req.title: System-Admin creates payroll-run
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to create a payroll-run through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers UI-driven payroll-run creation for system-admins. I derived tests using: EP for the happy-path with all valid mandatory fields; NEG for missing mandatory fields (400) and unauthenticated access (401) and forbidden role (403); BVA for a payroll-run name at the exact maximum allowed length; STC for the state transition from 'no payroll-run' to 'payroll-run created + audit record written'; DT for the combination of role and auth flags. Tests are written as pytest functions targeting the REST endpoint /payroll-runs so they remain deterministic and idempotent (unique run names via uuid, cleanup via fixture teardown).

tests.name: test_admin_creates_payroll_run_all_valid_fields
preconditions: No pre-existing payroll-run with the generated name
actions: POST /payroll-runs with all mandatory fields populated and valid admin auth token
expected: Audit record written for the new payroll-run
trace: R-WEB-037: audit record written on success
R-WEB-037: response 200 or 201 with entity id

tests.name: test_create_payroll_run_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
expected: Response body contains validation error details referencing 'period_start'
trace: R-WEB-037: error pathway - 400 on validation error
R-WEB-037: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_create_payroll_run_unauthenticated_returns_401
actions: POST /payroll-runs with all valid fields but without any auth token
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import api_post

def test_create_payroll_run_unauthenticated_returns_401():
    payload = {
        "name": "PR-no-auth",
        "period_start": "2024-01-01",
        "period_end": "2024-01-31",
        "pay_date": "2024-02-05",
        "department_id": <fixture>["department_id"]
    }
    resp = api_post("/payroll-runs", json=payload, token=None)
    assert resp.status_code == 401

tests.name: test_create_payroll_run_non_admin_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not admin)
actions: POST /payroll-runs with all valid fields using employee auth token
trace: R-WEB-037: error pathway - 403 if role forbidden

tests.name: test_create_payroll_run_name_at_max_length
preconditions: Maximum allowed name length is 100 characters (per field spec)
actions: POST /payroll-runs with a name field exactly 100 characters long
expected: Payroll-run is persisted with full 100-character name
trace: R-WEB-037: boundary - name at max allowed length must be accepted
R-WEB-037: positive outcome on valid boundary input
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from <helper_fn> import get_admin_token, api_post, api_get
from <helper_fn> import get_audit_record

MAX_NAME_LEN = 100

@pytest.fixture
def admin_token():
    return get_admin_token()

@pytest.fixture
def created_run_id(admin_token):
    ids = []
    yield ids
    for rid in ids:
        api_post("/payroll-runs/" + rid + "/cancel", token=admin_token)

def test_create_payroll_run_name_at_max_length(admin_token, created_run_id):
    base = "PR-BVA-" + str(uuid.uuid4()).replace("-", "")
    name = (base * 4)[:MAX_NAME_LEN]
    assert len(name) == MAX_NAME_LEN
    payload = {
        "name": name,
        "period_start": "2024-03-01",
        "period_end": "2024-03-31",
        "pay_date": "2024-04-05",
        "department_id": <fixture>["department_id"]
    }
    resp = api_post("/payroll-runs", json=payload, token=admin_token)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body and body["id"]
    created_run_id.append(body["id"])
    fetched = api_get("/payroll-runs/" + body["id"], token=admin_token).json()
    assert fetched["name"] == name

tests.name: test_create_payroll_run_name_exceeds_max_length_returns_400
preconditions: Maximum allowed name length is 100 characters (per field spec)
actions: POST /payroll-runs with a name field 101 characters long (max+1)
expected: Response body contains validation error referencing name length
trace: R-WEB-037: boundary - name at max+1 must be rejected
R-WEB-037: error pathway - 400 on validation error
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_admin_token, api_post

MAX_NAME_LEN = 100

@pytest.fixture
def admin_token():
    return get_admin_token()

def test_create_payroll_run_name_exceeds_max_length_returns_400(admin_token):
    name = "X" * (MAX_NAME_LEN + 1)
    assert len(name) == MAX_NAME_LEN + 1
    payload = {
        "name": name,
        "period_start": "2024-03-01",
        "period_end": "2024-03-31",
        "pay_date": "2024-04-05",
        "department_id": <fixture>["department_id"]
    }
    resp = api_post("/payroll-runs", json=payload, token=admin_token)
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    body = resp.json()
    errors = str(body).lower()
    assert "name" in errors, "Validation error must reference name field"

tests.name: test_payroll_run_state_transition_none_to_created_to_audit
preconditions: No existing payroll-run for this period
actions: Confirm no payroll-run exists for generated name (state: none)
POST /payroll-runs with valid payload (transition: none -> created)
GET /audit-records filtering on entity_id to verify audit entry (state: audit written)
expected: Initial GET confirms absence of entity
POST returns 200 or 201 with entity id
Audit record exists with action 'create' for the entity id
trace: R-WEB-037: state transition - none -> payroll-run created -> audit written
R-WEB-037: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from <helper_fn> import get_admin_token, api_post, api_get
from <helper_fn> import get_audit_record

@pytest.fixture
def admin_token():
    return get_admin_token()

@pytest.fixture
def created_run_id(admin_token):
    ids = []
    yield ids
    for rid in ids:
        api_post("/payroll-runs/" + rid + "/cancel", token=admin_token)

def test_payroll_run_state_transition_none_to_created_to_audit(admin_token, created_run_id):
    name = "PR-STC-" + str(uuid.uuid4())[:8]
    payload = {
        "name": name,
        "period_start": "2024-05-01",
        "period_end": "2024-05-31",
        "pay_date": "2024-06-05",
        "department_id": <fixture>["department_id"]
    }
    search = api_get("/payroll-runs?name=" + name, token=admin_token)
    assert search.json().get("total", 0) == 0, "Pre-state: entity must not exist"
    resp = api_post("/payroll-runs", json=payload, token=admin_token)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    run_id = resp.json()["id"]
    created_run_id.append(run_id)
    fetched = api_get("/payroll-runs/" + run_id, token=admin_token).json()
    assert fetched["status"] == "created"
    audit = get_audit_record(entity="payroll-run", entity_id=run_id, action="create")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist after creation"
